\n**Authentication**:\n\n1. Begin a conv:
**Authentication**:

1. Begin a conversation with the [Botfather](https://telegram.me/BotFather).
2. Type in "/newbot"
3. Choose a name for your bot
4. Choose a username for your bot.
5. Copy the token value from the Botfather and use it activepieces connection.
6. Congratulations! You can now use your new Telegram connection in your flows.

\n**How to obtain Chat ID:**\n1. Search:
**How to obtain Chat ID:**
1. Search for the bot "@getmyid_bot" in Telegram.
2. Start a conversation with the bot.
3. Send the command "/my_id" to the bot.
4. The bot will reply with your chat ID.

**Note: Remember to initiate the chat with the bot, or you'll get an error for "chat not found."**
Unique identifier for the target chat or: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel (e.g. @channelusername).
Unique identifier for the target message: Unique identifier for the target message thread of the forum; supergroup forums only.
How the message text should be parsed by: How the message text should be parsed by Telegram.
Disable link previews for links in this: このメッセージ内のリンクのリンクプレビューを無効にする.
Send the message silently. Users will re: Send the message silently. Users will receive a notification with no sound.
Protects the contents of the sent messag: Protects the contents of the sent message from forwarding and saving.
If the message is a reply, ID of the ori: If the message is a reply, ID of the original message.
Additional interface options. A JSON-ser: Additional interface options. A JSON-serialized object for an inline keyboard, custom reply keyboard, instructions to remove reply keyboard or to force a reply from the user.
The caption to send with the media: The caption to send with the media
The document file to send. Telegram supp: The document file to send. Telegram supports any file up to 50 MB.
Reuse a document previously uploaded to: Reuse a document previously uploaded to Telegram by passing its file_id. Either provide a document or a document id.
Optional caption to display below the do: Optional caption to display below the document (0–1024 chars).
The audio file to send (.MP3 or .M4A).
Reuse an audio file previously uploaded: Reuse an audio file previously uploaded to Telegram by passing its file_id.
Duration of the audio in seconds.
Radius of uncertainty for the location,: Radius of uncertainty for the location, in meters (0–1500).
Number of seconds the location will be u: Number of seconds the location will be updated (60–86400). Use for live locations.
For live locations, direction the user i: For live locations, direction the user is moving in degrees (1–360).
For live locations, the max distance (1–: For live locations, the max distance (1–100000 m) for proximity alerts about approaching another chat member.
Media items to send. Each must be a URL: Media items to send. Each must be a URL or a Telegram file_id. Mixing photo/video is allowed; audio and document groups cannot be mixed with other types.
List of answer options (2–10 options, ea: List of answer options (2–10 options, each 1–100 characters).
True, if the poll needs to be anonymous. Default true.
True if the poll allows multiple answers: True if the poll allows multiple answers (regular polls only).
0-based index of the correct answer opti: 0-based index of the correct answer option (required for quizzes).
Explanation shown when a user picks an i: Explanation shown when a user picks an incorrect answer (0–200 chars).
Amount of time in seconds the poll will: Amount of time in seconds the poll will be active (5–600).
Point in time when the poll will be auto: Point in time when the poll will be automatically closed.
Pass True if the poll should be immediat: Pass True if the poll should be immediately closed. Useful for previews.
Required when editing a message sent by: Required when editing a message sent by the bot. Leave empty to edit an inline message (use Inline Message Id instead).
Identifier of the message to edit (used: Identifier of the message to edit (used together with Chat Id).
Identifier of the inline message (mutual: Identifier of the inline message (mutually exclusive with Chat Id + Message Id).
New text of the message (1–4096 chars).
Identifier of the message to delete.
Chat the message will be forwarded to.
Chat the original message came from.
Identifier of the message to forward.
Identifier of the message to pin.
Pass True if it is not necessary to send: Pass True if it is not necessary to send a notification to all chat members.
Identifier of the pinned message to unpi: Identifier of the pinned message to unpin. Leave empty to unpin the most recent pinned message.
Unique identifier for the user: ユーザーの一意の識別子
File identifier to get information about: 情報を取得するためのファイル識別子
If enabled, the file is downloaded and r: If enabled, the file is downloaded and returned as base64.
Name of the invite link (max 32 chars): 招待リンクの名前（最大32文字）

If True, users joining the chat via the: If True, users joining the chat via the link need to be approved by chat administrators.
Unique identifier of the callback query. Available on `callback_query.id` in the New Callback Query trigger payload.
Text shown to the user. If not specified: Text shown to the user. If not specified, nothing is shown (the spinner is just stopped).
If True, an alert dialog is shown instea: If True, an alert dialog is shown instead of a notification at the top of the screen.

HEAD: 頭
Triggers when the bot receives a Telegra: Triggers when the bot receives a Telegram update (message, callback query, poll answer, etc.). One trigger per bot token — Telegram does not support multiple webhooks on the same bot.
\nTelegram allows only **one webhook per:
Telegram allows only **one webhook per bot token**, so a single Telegram trigger handles all update types for a given bot. Pick the update types this flow should listen for. Leave empty to use Telegram's default set (messages, edited channel posts, chat-member updates — **does not include callback queries**).

After selecting multiple types, use a Branch step downstream to fork on the update kind (e.g. `message` vs `callback_query`).
